ocr.text: In the Asia-Pacific pharmaceutical landscape, Novartis continues to demonstrate a robust commitment to advancing its research
and development (R&D) capabilities, as evidenced by the latest productivity metrics and partnership activities reported for Q3
R&DProductivityMetrics  TotalRevenue  NetIncome  R&DExpenses  CAPEX  ProfitMargin
Metric  Q32023  CNY  GrowthRate  R&DROI  NewDrugs  ClinicalTrials  PipelineValue
TotalRevenue  22.5B  5.1B  9.0B  3.8B  22.7%  15.0%  4
NetIncome  [HIDDEN]  21.5%  [HIDDEN]  16.5%  5  3  8.7B
R&DExpenses  9.0B  2.5B  [HIDDEN]  1.5B  [HIDDEN]  60%  [HIDDEN]
ProfitMargin  22.7%  15.0%  30.1%  [HIDDEN]  2.5%  4  Total
Notably, the company reported a Net Income of CNY 5.1B, reflecting a Growth Rate of 3.0B, which underscores its financial
resilience amidst a competitive market. Furthermore, Novartis has made significant strides in R&D Expenses, with a Pipeline
Value of 12 and a promising growth trajectory evidenced by a Growth Rate of 4.5B.
DealType  Amount(CNY)
LicensingDeals  Status
Partnership  150M  Active  Oncology
Collaboration  80M  Pending  Cardiology

The company's focus on innovation is also reflected in its New Drugs metric, which stands at an impressive 20.0%, indicating a
strong pipeline of upcoming therapies. In terms of strategic collaborations, Novartis has actively engaged in licensing and
partnership deals, with a notable Research Agreement valued at CNY 150M, aimed at enhancing its oncology portfolio. This is
aligned with the company's commitment to fostering partnerships that bolster its competitive advantage, as illustrated by its
active Joint Venture in the immunology sector. As Novartis continues to navigate the complexities of the Asia-Pacific market, its
focus on R&D productivity metrics such as R&D ROI, which stands at 5.3%, positions it favorably for future growth and
innovation.
